articles.title: Fed Holds Steady as Internal Split Highlights Growing Policy Uncertainty
articles.expand: Federal Reserve left its policy rate unchanged at 3.50–3.75%, in line with expectations, but the decision revealed a widening divide within the Committee. Governor Stephen Miran again dissented in favor of a 25bps cut, while Beth Hammack, Neel Kashkari, and Lorie Logan opposed the inclusion of an easing bias in the statement, signaling resistance t...

articles.title: EUR/CAD slides as steady BoC and rising Oil prices lift Loonie
articles.expand: EUR/CAD trades on the back foot on Wednesday as the Canadian Dollar (CAD) strengthens broadly following the Bank of Canada monetary policy announcement, while rising Oil prices add further support to the commodity-linked Loonie.

articles.title: Federal Reserve set to hold interest rates again in Powell’s last meeting as chair
articles.expand: The United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) announces its interest rate decision on Wednesday, another pivotal meeting for markets to gauge the stance of policymakers as energy prices remain uncomfortably high amid ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East, putting the Fed’s dual mandate under strain.

articles.title: NZDUSD trades to a new session low and tests 200 bar MA on the 4-hour chart
articles.expand: The NZDUSD is under pressure today, leading the majors to the downside against the USD. The pair is currently trading at 0.5834, down -0.87%, as a combination of risk-off sentiment and rising U.S. yields weighs on the currency. The broader market backdrop is not helping. The NASDAQ Composite has slipped into negative territory (down around -0.34%) ...

articles.title: April FOMC US Index Levels for Dow Jones, Nasdaq and S&P 500
articles.expand: Pre-FOMC Major US Indexes update: Wall Street braces for a pivotal 24 hours featuring Powell’s Fed swan song and heavy-hitter tech earnings. As the US-Iran impasse pushes Oil to $105, traders are questioning the market's resilience. Explore our pre-FOMC outlook for the Dow Jones, S P 500, and Nasdaq.

articles.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average slips below 49K ahead of Powell's final Fed rate call
articles.expand: Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures drifted lower through Wednesday's session, trading close to 48.8K at the time of writing after rejecting levels above 49.3K during the prior day.

articles.title: NZD/USD weakens as Fed policy caution, Iran tensions support US Dollar
articles.expand: NZD/USD trades lower around 0.5840 on Wednesday at the time of writing, down 0.76% on the day, as markets adopt a wait-and-see stance ahead of the Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy decision later in the day.

articles.title: BoC's Macklem: Inflation expectations are not as well anchored as they were before Covid
articles.expand: Tiff Macklem faced the press shortly after the decision, offering markets a clearer sense of the central bank’s thinking. The appearance followed the widely expected move by the Bank of Canada to hold the policy rate steady at 2.25%.

articles.title: Bank of Canada's Macklem: Under base case, changes to the policy rate likely to be minimal
articles.expand: The policy rate may still require adjustments depending on how various risks develop. Should the economy progress according to the base case, any changes to the policy rate will likely be minimal. Ongoing generalized inflation triggered by sustained high energy costs could necessitate consecutive rate hikes. Significant new trade restrictions from ...

articles.title: Gold holds near one-month lows as Oil-driven inflation and US-Iran tensions weigh
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades on the back foot on Wednesday, hovering near one-month lows ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy announcement due later in the American session at 18:00 GMT.

articles.title: AUD/USD slides as US Dollar gains on geopolitical tensions ahead of Fed decision
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar (AUD) edges lower against the US Dollar (USD) on Wednesday, weighed by softer-than-expected Australian inflation data, while fading hopes that the US-Iran war will end anytime soon support the Greenback.

articles.title: AUD/USD: Hawkish RBA path supports gains – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Deepali Bhargava and Francesco Pesole argue that rising energy-driven inflation and firm underlying price pressures strengthen the case for a 25bp Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) hike on 5 May.

articles.title: Gold: Rising reserve role as history returns – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank’s Mallika Sachdeva argues that shifting geopolitics are reshaping central bank reserve allocation towards Gold and away from the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Crude oil is racing higher and approaches a key target area. What next?
articles.expand: Crude oil futures are racing higher, up roughly $5 or 5% on the day, and pushing into a key decision zone. The move to the upside has been supported by reports that the Trump administration is speaking to oil companies about a extended blockade of the Strait of Hormuz that could last for months. The rally has brought price right up toward a swing/r...

articles.title: Bank of Canada neutral hold (2.25%) – USD/CAD rallies to 1.37 – Press Conference coming up
articles.expand: Bank of Canada and USD/CAD update: The Bank of Canada holds rates steady, citing a soft economic outlook based on a $75 oil assumption. With WTI Crude topping $100 and boosting Canada's national income, the BoC may soon pivot hawkish. As USD/CAD rallies to 1.37, explore our two-timeframe technical analysis.

articles.title: How and why all the Bank of Canada forecasts changed in the latest MPR
articles.expand: Here are the changes in today's MPR: GDP growth (annual) Essentially a wash on growth. The Bank explicitly states "the impact of higher global oil prices on economic activity is small." Government spending got revised up (provincial budgets), residential investment got revised down (affordability still biting). Net-zero. CPI inflation (annual) This...

articles.title: USDCAD moves to new session highs. Extends above 1.3700 and looks toward 1.3715
articles.expand: The USDCAD is pushing to new session highs after the Bank of Canada left rates unchanged, with the central bank signaling growing concern over a softening labor market. Inflation is expected to drift toward 3% in the near term before easing back early next year—assuming oil prices cooperate. That assumption on oil is not helping today. Crude oil is...

articles.title: GBP/USD falls as US Dollar gathers strength ahead of Fed interest rate decision
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair fell toward the 1.3480 price region on Wednesday as the US Dollar (USD) continued to gain strength ahead of the last Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate decision led by Chair Jerome Powell.

articles.title: BoC Holds at 2.25% as Oil-Driven Inflation Seen Temporary Despite Upgraded Outlook

articles.expand: Deutsche Bank’s Shreyas Gopal and Sanjay Raja note that EUR/GBP continues to trade above levels implied by Bank of England (BoE)-European Central Bank (ECB) rate differentials, leaving a measurable risk premium in the Pound (GBP).

articles.expand: Derek Halpenny at MUFG highlights that the Canadian Dollar (CAD) has held up well versus the US Dollar since the Middle East conflict began, with markets expecting the Bank of Canada (BoC) to keep rates unchanged but adopt a more hawkish tone.

articles.title: Australia’s inflation surge just made an RBA rate rise more likely
articles.expand: Australia’s inflation rate surged in March, driven by a record jump in fuel prices and making an interest rate hike next Tuesday more likely.

articles.title: Japanese Oil Tanker Crosses Hormuz Strait Without Paying Toll
articles.expand: A Japanese VLCC Idemitsu Maru carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi crude has passed through the Strait of Hormuz without paying Iran's toll, thanks to Japan's diplomatic negotiations.

articles.title: NZ sharemarket flat, up only 0.05% as investors wait
articles.expand: The New Zealand sharemarket was flat as investors waited for a rush of important earnings results from the US and the Federal Reserve’s latest stance on inflation and interest rates. The S&P/NZX 50 Index traded in a narrow range o f 12,726.35 and 12,783.97 before closing at 12,770.3, up 5.9 points or 0.05%.
